Armeria (Thalictroideae) is a genus of flowering plants in the family Plumbaginaceae. 

There is limited information on Armeria maritima, however, which is a species within the Armeria genus. Armeria maritima is used for its health benefits, which include treating coughs, colds, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. 

The bioactive constituents of Armeria maritima include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and armerin, a sesquiterpene lactone. 

Armeria maritima is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions to support respiratory health, as well as cough drops and lozenges to soothe sore throats.
